_publications._publicationTitle: Quality of life in patients with neurofibromatosis type 1 and 2 in Canada.

_contextSnippet: The PROMIS pain interference scores had moderate to high correlations with the SF-36 PCS, EQ-5D-5L health utilities, global health VAS, and the NFTI-QoL pain item. When analyzed separately by type of NF, the correlation between pain interference and health utilities was −0.70 (CI: −0.77, −0.61, P < .001) for NF1 and −0.90 (CI: −0.96, −0.78, P < .001) for NF2
details: Moderate to high correlations with SF-36 PCS, EQ-5D-5L health utilities, global health VAS, and NFTI-QoL pain item. Correlation with health utilities was -0.70 for NF1 and -0.90 for NF2 (P < 0.001 for both).
